description: A resilient scraping utility capable of handling URLs with randomized delays and detecting anti-bot protections/CAPTCHAs to trigger fallback workflows.

userStories.title: Basic URL HTML Retrieval
userStories.description: As a system coordinator, I want to fetch raw HTML from a given job URL so that the HTML can be sent to the ingestion parser.
acceptanceCriteria: Scenario: Successfully fetching HTML from a valid URL
Given a valid job URL
When the scraper is called
Then it returns the raw HTML text of the page
Scenario: Handling invalid URLs
Given an invalid or unreachable URL (e.g., 404, DNS error)
When the scraper is called
Then it raises a generic ScraperError that the ingestion node can catch

tasks.description: Create src/utils/scraper.py with basic ScraperError exception class
Write fetch_html function using httpx or requests with standard user-agent headers
Implement basic error handling for 4xx/5xx status codes (excluding 403 for now)
Write pytest unit tests for successful fetch and 404/DNS errors

userStories.notes: Keep dependencies minimal, e.g., requests or httpx.

userStories.title: Randomized Delays & Rate Limit Handling
userStories.description: As a system coordinator, I want the scraper to use randomized delays and handle 429 responses so that it mimics human behavior and avoids triggering simple rate limits.
acceptanceCriteria: Scenario: Adding random delays before requests
Given the scraper is about to make a request
When it executes
Then it pauses for a random interval (e.g., 2-5 seconds) before making the request
Scenario: Handling HTTP 429 Too Many Requests
Given a target URL that responds with an HTTP 429
When the scraper receives this response
Then it automatically retries with exponential backoff up to a maximum limit before failing

tasks.description: Implement a configurable delay mechanism using time.sleep and random.uniform
Wrap the HTTP request in a retry mechanism (e.g., tenacity) specifically for HTTP 429 responses
Add logging to track delays, retries, and failures
Write pytest unit tests simulating a 429 response and verifying the retry logic and backoff

userStories.notes: Ensure tests mock time.sleep to avoid slowing down the test suite.

userStories.title: Anti-Bot & CAPTCHA Detection
userStories.description: As a system coordinator, I want the scraper to detect when it has been blocked by a CAPTCHA or 403 Forbidden so that the system can cleanly fail and prompt the user for manual fallback.
acceptanceCriteria: Scenario: Detecting HTTP 403 Forbidden
Given a target URL that responds with an HTTP 403
When the scraper makes the request
Then it immediately raises a specific AntiBotBlockError without retrying
Scenario: Detecting in-page CAPTCHAs
Given a target URL that returns a 200 OK but the HTML contains common CAPTCHA/Auth wall signatures
When the scraper processes the returned HTML
Then it detects the signature and raises the AntiBotBlockError

tasks.description: Create a custom AntiBotBlockError exception class
Update the fetch function to intercept 403 status codes and raise AntiBotBlockError
Add logic to inspect returned HTML text for common CAPTCHA keywords (e.g., 'Cloudflare', 'authwall', 'security check', 'captcha')
Write pytest unit tests using mocked HTML responses to trigger and assert the AntiBotBlockError

userStories.notes: AntiBotBlockError must be distinct from generic ScraperErrors so the Parent Graph knows to switch job status to MANUAL_INPUT_REQUIRED.
